module1.clean_text:
HOSPITALIZATION RECORD:
This is the child [PATIENT] aged [AGE] years and [AGE] originally from and residing in EL MEGHAIR, from a middle-aged non-consanguineous couple, admitted to our level for the management of a syndromic PID type Hyper IgE
Reason for hospitalization: Update
HISTORY:
Family:
Father: [AGE] years old, BES. Mother: [AGE] years old, BES. Siblings: daughter age [AGE] in BES, screening done
Inbreeding: no
Atopy: yes
Chronic respiratory disease in the family: no
Personal:
Physiological:
Pregnancy: well monitored
Delivery: full term vaginally
Apgar 10/10Neonatal resuscitation: no
Birth weight: 3.2kg
Meconium: emitted<24h
No delay in umbilical cord fall
Breastfeeding: maternal then mixed from the age of [AGE] Vaccination: Health record not returned
Vitamin D received at the age of [AGE] Diversification at the age of [AGE] psychomotor development for the age
Attended 2nd year of primary school with average academic performance
Socio-economic level: good
Personal atopy: no
Pathological:
Syndromic primary immunodeficiency type Hyper IgE since [DATE] (age [AGE] monitored at Parnet University Hospital
History of the disease:
Date of diagnosis: [DATE] of diagnosis: [AGE] years
Diagnostic elements:
Clinic:
Eczematous lesions
Multiple recurrent skin abscesses
Paraclinic:
- Immunodeficiency assessment: not available
NUMBER OF CURES RECEIVED: 14 courses of venoglobulins:
- Parnet University Hospital: 06 courses of venoglobulins
- EPH El-meghair: 08 courses of venoglobulins
the last of which was on [DATE] at EPH El-meghair. PROCESSING IN PROGRESS:
Bactrim (40/200) at preventive dose
- The history of this episode: dates back to 10 days before his admission to our level, by the appearance of a perianal swelling with an inflammatory appearance of approximately 03cm which corresponds to an abscess, an oral antibiotic treatment was prescribed based on bactrim + fusidic acid with a slight improvement. The child was referred to our service for further care. CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION:
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 31kg (+2.02 DS)
Size: 122cm (+0.37DS)
BMI: 20.8(+2.37SD)
Gomez score: 137%
Waterloo Score: 134%
General condition: preserved, T° 36.7 c, dextro: 1.06 g/l
Skin examination: good mucosal skin coloring, BCG scar present, Cutaneous xerosis with pruritus scar lesion
ENT examination: no cervical ADP, clean throat. Pleuropulmonary examination:
FR 25 c/min
	No signs of respiratory distress. SPO2: 98% AA
Pulmonary auscultation: clear at 2CP
No signs of chronic respiratory failure (clubbing), no chest deformity. Cardiovascular examination: HR: 110 b/min. Auscultation: B1B2 audible no murmurs or BSA
Digestive examination: soft abdomen no organomegaly, transit preserved: 1 stool/day with normal appearance. Proctological examination: presence of an uncollected right perianal abscess fistulized externally
Urogenital examination: Female morphotype, no anomalies of sexual differentiation
Review of other devices: is unremarkable
BIOLOGY:
Correct NFS: GB: 5190 (PNN: 2020 / LYMP: 1670) HB: 12.2 PLQT: 287000
CRP: Negative VS accelerated: 21/54
Procalcitonin negative: <0.05
TP: 89% FIBRINOGEN: 3.20
UREA: 0.17 CREATININE: 6.05
Na: 134 K: 3.96 Cl: 109
TGO: 21 U/L TGP: 30.6
TSH: 1.284 FT3: 4.04 FT4: 12.24
VITAMIN D low: 13.6 ng/ml
Calcemia: 93.3 mg/l
ECBU: negative
Stool parasitology / stool culture: negative
ECB from abscess pus: Enterobacteriaceae SPP
RADIOLOGY:
Ultrasound of soft parts (03/11/2025): Subcutaneous formation next to the right gluteal fold, well limited by a thick wall, measuring 31x16mm with echogenic content. THORACIC CT (05/11/2025): dilation of the distal bronchi
CAT:
Hospitalization. Conditioning. VVP. Complete assessment
Monitoring: CHMD, blood sugar, temperature. Dietary interview
Placement on antibiotic therapy for 10 days adapted to the antibiogram
Emollient treatment + local treatment of the abscess
Bactrim + itraconazole: preventive dose
Vitamin D supplementation
Evolution:
During his hospitalization which lasted 12 days:
Clinical: marked improvement of the perianal abscess
Ultrasound of soft parts (12/11/2025): Regression of the 18mm collection
She received her 15th treatment on 11/12/2025, which passed without incident.
CONCLUSION
This is the MANSOURI child aged 07, originally from and residing in El Meghair, from a non-consanguineous middle-aged couple, with a personal history of a PID type syndromic immunodeficiency type hyper IgE admitted to our service for the management of a perianal abscess with a marked improvement, which requires regular courses of venoglobulins at a rate of 0.6g/kg/d every 04 weeks
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Retrieve the medical report from CHU Parnet
- Retrieve the medical report from EPH elmeghair
- Retrieve the patient's immune deficiency assessment
- Referral to specialist consultation

HOSPITALIZATION RECORD:
This is the child MANSOURI CHAIMA aged 06 years and 11 months, originally from and residing in EL MEGHAIR, from a middle-aged non-consanguineous couple, admitted to our level for the management of a syndromic PID type Hyper IgE
Reason for hospitalization: Update
HISTORY:
Family:
Father: 45 years old, BES. Mother: 36 years old, BES. Siblings: daughter age 09 in BES, screening done
Inbreeding: no
Atopy: yes
Chronic respiratory disease in the family: no
Personal:
Physiological:
Pregnancy: well monitored
Delivery: full term vaginally
Apgar 10/10Neonatal resuscitation: no
Birth weight: 3.2kg
Meconium: emitted<24h
No delay in umbilical cord fall
Breastfeeding: maternal then mixed from the age of 3 months. Vaccination: Health record not returned
Vitamin D received at the age of 01 months/06 months. Diversification at the age of 6 months
good psychomotor development for the age
Attended 2nd year of primary school with average academic performance
Socio-economic level: good
Personal atopy: no
Pathological:
Syndromic primary immunodeficiency type Hyper IgE since July 2022 (age 02) monitored at Parnet University Hospital
History of the disease:
Date of diagnosis: 07/2024
Age of diagnosis: 02 years
Diagnostic elements:
Clinic:
Eczematous lesions
Multiple recurrent skin abscesses
Paraclinic:
- Immunodeficiency assessment: not available
NUMBER OF CURES RECEIVED: 14 courses of venoglobulins:
- Parnet University Hospital: 06 courses of venoglobulins
- EPH El-meghair: 08 courses of venoglobulins
the last of which was on 10/27/2025 at EPH El-meghair. PROCESSING IN PROGRESS:
Bactrim (40/200) at preventive dose
- The history of this episode: dates back to 10 days before his admission to our level, by the appearance of a perianal swelling with an inflammatory appearance of approximately 03cm which corresponds to an abscess, an oral antibiotic treatment was prescribed based on bactrim + fusidic acid with a slight improvement. The child was referred to our service for further care. CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION:
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 31kg (+2.02 DS)
Size: 122cm (+0.37DS)
BMI: 20.8(+2.37SD)
Gomez score: 137%
Waterloo Score: 134%
General condition: preserved, T° 36.7 c, dextro: 1.06 g/l
Skin examination: good mucosal skin coloring, BCG scar present, Cutaneous xerosis with pruritus scar lesion
ENT examination: no cervical ADP, clean throat. Pleuropulmonary examination:
FR 25 c/min
	No signs of respiratory distress. SPO2: 98% AA
Pulmonary auscultation: clear at 2CP
No signs of chronic respiratory failure (clubbing), no chest deformity. Cardiovascular examination: HR: 110 b/min. Auscultation: B1B2 audible no murmurs or BSA
Digestive examination: soft abdomen no organomegaly, transit preserved: 1 stool/day with normal appearance. Proctological examination: presence of an uncollected right perianal abscess fistulized externally
Urogenital examination: Female morphotype, no anomalies of sexual differentiation
Review of other devices: is unremarkable
BIOLOGY:
Correct NFS: GB: 5190 (PNN: 2020 / LYMP: 1670) HB: 12.2 PLQT: 287000
CRP: Negative VS accelerated: 21/54
Procalcitonin negative: <0.05
TP: 89% FIBRINOGEN: 3.20
UREA: 0.17 CREATININE: 6.05
Na: 134 K: 3.96 Cl: 109
TGO: 21 U/L TGP: 30.6
TSH: 1.284 FT3: 4.04 FT4: 12.24
VITAMIN D low: 13.6 ng/ml
Calcemia: 93.3 mg/l
ECBU: negative
Stool parasitology / stool culture: negative
ECB from abscess pus: Enterobacteriaceae SPP
RADIOLOGY:
Ultrasound of soft parts (03/11/2025): Subcutaneous formation next to the right gluteal fold, well limited by a thick wall, measuring 31x16mm with echogenic content. THORACIC CT (05/11/2025): dilation of the distal bronchi
CAT:
Hospitalization. Conditioning. VVP. Complete assessment
Monitoring: CHMD, blood sugar, temperature. Dietary interview
Placement on antibiotic therapy for 10 days adapted to the antibiogram
Emollient treatment + local treatment of the abscess
Bactrim + itraconazole: preventive dose
Vitamin D supplementation
Evolution:
During his hospitalization which lasted 12 days:
Clinical: marked improvement of the perianal abscess
Ultrasound of soft parts (12/11/2025): Regression of the 18mm collection
She received her 15th treatment on 11/12/2025, which passed without incident.
CONCLUSION
This is the MANSOURI child aged 07, originally from and residing in El Meghair, from a non-consanguineous middle-aged couple, with a personal history of a PID type syndromic immunodeficiency type hyper IgE admitted to our service for the management of a perianal abscess with a marked improvement, which requires regular courses of venoglobulins at a rate of 0.6g/kg/d every 04 weeks
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Retrieve the medical report from CHU Parnet
- Retrieve the medical report from EPH elmeghair
- Retrieve the patient's immune deficiency assessment
- Referral to specialist consultation
